Sleuth's recognize that the consistency principle is activated by prior commitments, and that it is amplified when those prior commitments have been made actively, publicly, and voluntarily. By asking the right questions and doing their homework to uncover what their audience has already chosen, Sleuth's then shape their requests to align with these prior commitments. To influence others, Sleuth's commissioned the force of the principle of consistency by pointing out how their proposals are aligned with active, public commitments that have already been made, and also by giving people the freedom to choose among those options. The key questions Sleuth's ask themselves are, have I done my homework to uncover the prior choices and stands that my audience has made publicly, actively, and voluntarily? Also, have I asked for initial commitments from others to be made in an active, public, and voluntary way so that the others will be more likely to stay true to them?
